name: Eggplant Casserole
recipeInstructions:
For corn bread layer: Sprinkle 1 1/2 cups crumbled cornbread in the bottom of an ungreased 11 x 7-inch baking dish.
For eggplant layer: Slice eggplant into 1/4-inch thick slices. Cut large slices in half. In a large skillet, sauté eggplant in oil for 3 to 4 minutes on each side. Drain on absorbent paper. Sprinkle with salt. Pour all but 1 tablespoon of oil from the skillet.
For meat layer: In the same skillet, cook ground beef, onion, and garlic until brown. Drain fat. Add tomato sauce, wine, cinnamon, salt, and oregano; mix well. Set aside.
For topping: In a medium saucepan, melt butter. Add flour, salt, and nutmeg. Cook over medium heat for 2 minutes. Add milk; cook and stir about 1 minute or until thickened. Stir in eggs; return to milk mixture. Add cheeses; stir until melted. Remove from heat.
To assemble casserole: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Layer half of the eggplant slices over the cornbread layer. Spread meat mixture over eggplant. Layer with remaining 1 cup crumbled cornbread. Layer remaining eggplant slices over cornbread. Pour topping over eggplant. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes or until golden brown. Makes about 8 servings.
Corn Bread:
Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C). Grease an 8-inch square baking pan. In a medium bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Add milk, egg, and oil; mix just until dry ingredients are moistened. Pour into prepared baking dish. Bake in the preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.
